interpretation: K=8 realised-trajectory analysis. The actual log_2 drift over a window starting at integer n exceeds the formula drift by log_2(1 + B_pi/(3^m * n)). At K=8 the maximum excess across all 16.7M fibers is 1.245 (small-n fibers near affine fixed points). 338,665 / 12,711,807 = 2.66% of edges violate the iteration-068 psi when using drift_actual_max. The re-solved LP with drift_actual was killed after 27 min CPU without convergence; the K=6 analog (203,615 edges) returned INFEASIBLE in 0.3s, and we expect K=8 to behave similarly. The qualitative conclusion stands: the iteration-068 fixed-lambda certificate is asymptotic / formula-based, not realised-trajectory feasible. A genuine Collatz descent proof in this framework would require either a state functional sensitive to actual n value (not just (r, pi)), or a hybrid with arbitrary-precision boundary verification on small n.
